# One-Day Meal Plan: Multi-Stage Cycling Tour
**Athlete Profile:** 180cm, 68kg | Goal: Rapid glycogen replenishment, sustained energy, gluten-free, fully portable

**Estimated needs:** ~3,800-4,200 kcal | 7-9g carbs/kg (500-600g) | 1.6-2g protein/kg (110-135g) | Moderate fat

---

## PRE-STAGE (2-3 hours before start)
**Rice & Egg Breakfast Bowl (portable container)**
- 1.5 cups cooked white rice
- 2 scrambled eggs
- 1/2 banana, sliced
- 1 tbsp honey drizzled on top
- Pinch of salt

*~650 kcal | 110g carbs | 20g protein — easy-digesting, tops off liver glycogen*

---

## DURING STAGE 1 (on-bike fueling, every 20-30 min)
- 2 GF energy bars (rice-based, e.g., oat/rice crisp bars) — 400 kcal
- 1 bottle sports drink (electrolyte + carb mix) — 150 kcal
- 1 banana — 105 kcal
- Handful of dried mango or dates — 200 kcal

*~850 kcal | 190g carbs — target 60-90g carbs/hour*

---

## IMMEDIATELY POST-STAGE 1 (within 30 min — critical glycogen window)
**Recovery Shake (portable shaker bottle)**
- GF whey or pea protein isolate (30g protein)
- 1 cup lactose-free milk or GF oat milk
- 1 ripe banana
- 1 tbsp maple syrup
- Pinch of salt

*~450 kcal | 70g carbs | 30g protein — 3:1 carb-to-protein ratio for rapid resynthesis*

---

## BETWEEN STAGES (packed lunch, portable)
**Rice Paper Wraps (Vietnamese-style, no cooking needed)**
- Rice paper wraps filled with: shredded chicken, cooked rice noodles, shredded carrot, cucumber, avocado
- GF tamari dipping sauce
- Side: GF pretzels or rice crackers with hummus
- 1 cup mixed berries

*~750 kcal | 100g carbs | 35g protein*

---

## PRE-STAGE 2 (30-45 min before, if same day)
- GF rice cakes with honey and thin banana slices
- Small handful of pretzels + electrolyte sip

*~300 kcal | 65g carbs — quick top-up*

---

## EVENING RECOVERY (after final stage, portable/easy assembly)
**GF Pasta & Protein Bowl**
- 2 cups GF brown rice pasta
- Grilled chicken or salmon (5-6oz)
- Roasted vegetables (zucchini, bell peppers, s

output:
# One-Day Meal Plan: Cutting Cut (84kg → 77kg in 5 Days)

## Context & Strategy Notes
Losing 7kg in 5 days safely means combining **modest fat loss + significant water/glycogen manipulation** toward the end of the window (last 24-48 hrs pre-weigh-in typically drop water/sodium/carbs hard). This Day 1 plan is a **moderate deficit, high-protein, low-sodium, whole-food plan** to preserve muscle while starting the taper. Adjust water-cut protocols closer to weigh-in day separately.

**Targets for today:** ~2,200-2,300 kcal | Protein ~190g | Sodium <1,500mg | Carbs moderate (training day) | All whole foods, cooked from scratch (no salt added — flavor via herbs, citrus, garlic, vinegar)

---

## Eating Window: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM

### 🕛 Meal 1 – 12:00 PM (Post-Fast Breaking Meal)
**Grilled Chicken & Sweet Potato Bowl**
- 200g grilled chicken breast (seasoned with black pepper, paprika, garlic, lemon)
- 250g roasted sweet potato (olive oil, rosemary)
- 150g steamed broccoli
- 1 tbsp olive oil drizzle
- Fresh lemon juice to taste

*~650 kcal | 55g protein | ~90mg sodium (natural)*

---

### 🕒 Meal 2 – 3:00 PM (Pre/Post-Training Fuel)
**Beef & Rice with Vegetables**
*(Time around your training session for best performance)*
- 180g lean ground beef or sirloin, pan-seared (garlic, cumin, black pepper)
- 200g cooked white rice
- 100g sautéed spinach (garlic, splash of vinegar)
- 1/2 avocado

*~700 kcal | 50g protein | ~120mg sodium*

---

### 🕕 Meal 3 – 6:00 PM (Recovery Meal)
**Baked Salmon with Quinoa**
- 200g baked salmon (dill, lemon, black pepper — no salt)
- 150g cooked quinoa
- 150g roasted asparagus
- Squeeze of fresh lime

*~600 kcal | 45g protein | ~110mg sodium (fish naturally contains some sodium)*

---

### 🕖 Meal 4 – 7:30 PM (Light Closing Snack Before Fast)
**Greek Yogurt & Berries**
- 200g plain, unsweetened Greek yogurt
- 100g mixed berries
- 15g raw almonds or walnuts
- Cinnamon to taste

*~300 kcal | 25g protein | ~60mg sodium*

---

## Daily Totals (Approximate)
| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|

However, the mandatory requirement of including a daily caloric total and macronutrient breakdown is only partially satisfied—the intro gives target ranges (kcal, protein, sodium) but the actual 'Daily Totals' table meant to sum up real achieved values is truncated and only shows the header row with no data.
